metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students examine fundamental economic principles governing how societies allocate resources and make economic decisions. This course covers microeconomic and macroeconomic concepts, with emphasis on Canadian and global contexts, and explores how economic policy affects individuals and organizations.
metadata.general_description: Microeconomics, macroeconomics, supply and demand, fiscal policy, and Canadian and global economic systems.

big_ideas.statement: Economics analyzes how individuals, businesses, and governments make decisions about allocating limited resources
big_ideas.elaboration: Because resources are finite, every society must determine what to produce, how to produce it, and who receives the output.

big_ideas.statement: Supply and demand interactions in markets determine prices and quantities of goods and services
big_ideas.elaboration: Market equilibrium reflects the balance between what producers are willing to supply and what consumers are willing to demand.

big_ideas.statement: Macroeconomic factors including employment, inflation, and economic growth affect entire economies and populations
big_ideas.elaboration: Government policies influence these aggregate factors, with ripple effects on inflation rates, unemployment, and standards of living.

big_ideas.statement: Global trade, exchange rates, and international economic relationships interconnect national economies
big_ideas.elaboration: Canada's economy exists within a global system where international trade, investment, and financial flows shape domestic opportunities.

competencies.raw: Design economic solutions that address real-world resource allocation problems
i_can_statements: I can identify economic problems facing individuals, businesses, or communities
I can analyze costs and benefits of different solutions
I can propose economic policies or strategies to address challenges
I can evaluate effectiveness of proposed solutions

competencies.raw: Design market strategies based on supply and demand principles
i_can_statements: I can analyze market conditions and price elasticity
I can forecast demand for products or services
I can develop pricing strategies based on market dynamics
I can identify market opportunities and threats

competencies.raw: Design financial plans considering macroeconomic conditions
i_can_statements: I can assess economic conditions and their personal impacts
I can plan investments considering inflation and interest rates
I can make employment decisions based on economic forecasts
I can develop strategies to manage economic uncertainty

competencies.raw: Design business strategies considering international economic factors
i_can_statements: I can analyze international trade opportunities and risks
I can evaluate currency exchange rate impacts on business
I can develop strategies for global market participation
I can assess tariff and trade policy implications

competencies.raw: Apply microeconomic concepts to analyze consumer and business decisions
i_can_statements: I can analyze how consumers respond to price changes
I can evaluate business profit-maximizing decisions
I can calculate opportunity costs in decision-making
I can apply utility theory to consumer behavior

competencies.raw: Apply macroeconomic analysis to understand national economic performance
i_can_statements: I can calculate and interpret gross domestic product
I can analyze unemployment rates and labor market trends
I can evaluate inflation impacts on purchasing power
I can assess economic growth and business cycle phases

competencies.raw: Apply monetary and fiscal policy concepts to economic situations
i_can_statements: I can analyze central bank policy decisions and effects
I can evaluate government taxation and spending policies
I can assess impacts of policy changes on inflation and employment
I can predict policy transmission mechanisms

competencies.raw: Apply international economics to global business and trade situations
i_can_statements: I can analyze comparative advantage and trade benefits
I can evaluate impacts of currency exchange rates
I can assess trade agreements and protectionist policies
I can analyze international capital flows and investment

competencies.raw: Use data analysis tools to examine economic information
i_can_statements: I can access and interpret economic data from StatsCan and other sources
I can use spreadsheets to analyze economic trends
I can create visualizations of economic data
I can conduct statistical analysis of economic relationships

competencies.raw: Use economic forecasting and modeling tools
i_can_statements: I can build spreadsheet models to forecast economic scenarios
I can analyze sensitivity of outcomes to different assumptions
I can use time-series analysis for trend prediction
I can interpret economic forecasts from major institutions

competencies.raw: Use financial technology to access economic information and manage finances
i_can_statements: I can use financial websites and apps to track markets
I can access economic news and analysis from reputable sources
I can use investment and banking technologies
I can evaluate cryptocurrency and emerging financial technologies

competencies.raw: Use presentation technology to communicate economic analysis
i_can_statements: I can create visualizations that communicate economic concepts
I can build dashboards to display key economic indicators
I can present economic analysis using multimedia tools
I can use simulations to demonstrate economic principles

areas.name: Microeconomic Foundations and Market Analysis
topics: Supply and demand analysis and market equilibrium

areas.name: Macroeconomics and National Economy
topics: Gross domestic product and economic growth measurement
Inflation, deflation, and price level changes
Money supply, interest rates, and financial markets

areas.name: Government Policy and Economic Management
topics: Monetary policy and central bank operations

areas.name: International Economics and Global Systems
topics: Exchange rates and foreign exchange markets
